behavior_summary: Handles the 'got hit' event for a floor-spike object: when struck the spike may play an effect/animation, change its visual type/state, possibly fling (hurl) a struck actor/NPC with randomized direction, and then disable itself.
The handler first guards on the spike's quantity (Item::getQ) and returns immediately if zero. If non-zero it forces quantity to 1 (Item::setQ) to ensure the hit processing runs.

Behavior splits on the second argument passed to the event: when that argument is zero the spike follows a simple spawn+frame-animation path (sets type 0x0339, plays effects/frames, then sets type 0x033A and clears quantity).

When the second argument is non-zero the code attempts a target-specific branch: if the item refers to an NPC and that NPC is alive it invokes an NPC-specific method (METHOD::0080) with randomized parameters; otherwise it computes randomized velocities and calls Item::hurl to fling the target. Both variants then run spawn/frame animations and finalize by changing type to 0x033A and setting quantity to 0.
notable_control_flow: Early guard: Item::getQ == 0 -> immediate return (prevents repeated activation).
Initial normalization: Item::setQ(this, 1) is called to ensure single-hit processing.
Primary branch by second parameter (word): value == 0 -> 'type 0x0339' path; value != 0 -> 'targeted hurl/interaction' path.
Animation loop: repeated calls to spawn FREE::1C19 and Item::setFrame interleaved with suspend/ticks to animate frames over several iterations.
Post-effect cleanup: Item::setType(this, 0x033A) and Item::setQ(this, 0) clear the spike for future interactions.
open_questions: The semantic meaning of numeric type IDs 0x0339 and 0x033A (exact visuals/state names) is inferred but not concretely known.
The provenance of the second parameter (BP+0Ah): whether it is always an attacker PID, a boolean flag, or sometimes another identifier is not fully certain.
Exact behavior and side effects of METHOD::0080 when called for live NPCs are unclear; it likely applies an impulse but implementation details are unknown.
Precise semantics of the spawn prototypes FREE::2A12 and FREE::1C19 (what visual/sound they produce) are inferred from usage patterns rather than explicit identifiers.

main_function.name: U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to the spike being hit: play spawn/effect animations, possibly fling a struck actor/NPC with randomized impulse, change the spike's visual/type state, and disable it (clear quantity).
entry_conditions: Invoked by the item's 'got hit' event handler.
Only proceeds if Item::getQ(this) != 0 (otherwise returns immediately).

function_map.name: U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit
function_map.role: Primary event handler for spike being struck; coordinates guard, branching, animation, optional hurl, and cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::getQ
function_map.role: Read the spike's quantity to decide whether to process the hit.

function_map.name: Item::setQ
function_map.role: Set/normalize the spike quantity at start (to 1) and clear it at end (to 0) to control re-triggering.

function_map.name: Item::setType
function_map.role: Swap the spike's type/visual state (used with values 0x0339 and 0x033A during hit lifecycle).

function_map.name: spawn FREE::2A12
function_map.role: Spawn a larger effect at the start of the hit sequence (visual/sound effect).

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn a short-duration per-frame effect or sound used inside the animation loop.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Set the item's animation/display frame during the animation loop.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Query whether the referenced item refers to an NPC to select NPC-specific handling.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Check NPC life state to avoid interacting with dead NPCs.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Produce small random numbers used to compute randomized hurl impulses/angles.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: An inlined/engine method called for live NPC targets with computed args; likely applies specialized NPC impulse or reaction.

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Apply a physical hurl/launch to a non-NPC target using computed x/y velocities/angles.

external_calls.purpose: Guard: return early if quantity is zero.
Normalize to quantity 1 at start of processing and clear quantity (0) at end to disable spike.
Switch visual/state: set to 0x0339 on immediate-hit branch, then to 0x033A at completion.
Create an initial hit effect at the spike's location.
Play repeated short per-frame effects/sounds during the animation loop.
Animate the spike over several frames during the hit sequence.
Determine whether to take an NPC-specific interaction branch.
Check for NPC being alive before applying NPC-specific method/impulse.
Generate randomized components for hurl/impulse vectors.
Invoke NPC-specific interaction (likely apply impulse/reaction) for live NPC targets with computed random parameters.
Apply a randomized hurl/launch to non-NPC targets (x/y velocity arguments computed from urandom).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'got hit' event for a floor-spike object: when struck the spike may play an effect/animation, change its visual type/state, possibly fling (hurl) a struck actor/NPC with randomized direction, and then disable itself.
